title: Chicken Jalfrezi

overview.description: A fresh, lightly spiced stir-fried chicken curry with peppers, onions and tomatoes. Brighter and drier than many restaurant versions.

ingredients: 700g chicken breast, cut into bite-sized pieces
method: Heat the ghee over a medium-high heat.
Add the cumin seeds and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Cook for 6–8 minutes until beginning to soften but still retaining some bite.
Add the garlic, ginger and green chillies.
Stir in the ground coriander, cumin, turmeric and Kashmiri chilli powder.
Add the chicken and cook until lightly browned.
Cook for 8–10 minutes until the tomatoes soften into a light sauce and the chicken is cooked through.
Finish with the garam masala and fresh coriander.

learning.did-you-know: The word 'jalfrezi' comes from Bengali and roughly means 'hot fry'. The dish was originally created to transform leftover cooked meat into a new meal.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to bloom spices, cook vegetables so they retain texture and create a curry with a light, fresh sauce rather than a heavy gravy.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: Offers a completely different style of Indian curry, with crisp vegetables and a lighter sauce that's ideal for a weekday meal.
chapman-tips: Don't overcook the peppers—they should still have a little bite.
Chicken breast works well here because the cooking time is short.
Adjust the number of green chillies to suit your family's heat preference.
Traditionally, this dish can also be made with leftover roast chicken.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze once cooled in family-sized portions.
notes.leftovers: Excellent reheated and also works well wrapped in homemade chapatis for lunch.
